title: Pelican to Murder — Chapter 2: The Sponsor Gap
description: After the first murder, detectives follow the Black Pelican thread into fake sponsor money, access logs, and a manager whose alibi cracks under the timeline. The case is harder because the killer uses a staged gap instead of one obvious object.

answerKey.method: Switched Lena’s allergy-safe drink and hid her injector
answerKey.motive: To stop Lena from exposing the stolen sponsor money
answerKey.keyEvidence: Camera export gap, safe access log, and fake sponsor invoices
answerKey.explanation: Owen had the financial motive, the office access, and the ability to alter both the drink path and the camera export. The safe log and fake invoices point to the sponsor theft, while the camera gap proves he tried to hide the moment that connects him to the switch.

publicClues.title: Deleted Charity Tag
publicClues.text: At 9:08 PM, a recovered invoice draft shows Bayou Night Logistics attached to a charity livestream note. The money trail did not stop with Owen.

rounds.liveActionTitle: The office door becomes a problem.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: Owen insists he was “buried in paperwork,” but a server says the office door opened right after Lena asked about sponsor receipts. Owen stops correcting people and starts checking who heard that.

rounds.title: Final Accusation

rounds.countdownReview: The final window is closing. Submit all answers before the case locks.
rounds.breakingUpdate: Final round evidence is live. One detail should help separate the loudest suspect from the provable answer.

openingNarration: The Black Pelican line from Chapter 1 leads to sponsor invoices, office access, and a missing injector. This chapter is still a complete murder case, but the motive now stretches beyond jealousy. Someone used the confusion around Lena’s allergy to protect stolen money.
revealScript:
Case closed. This was Level 2: Rookie Detective Case. The culprit was Owen Landry. The method: Switched Lena’s allergy-safe drink and hid her injector. The motive: To stop Lena from exposing the stolen sponsor money. The proof: Camera export gap, safe access log, and fake sponsor invoices.

Continuing Story Thread: Owen is exposed, but his fake invoice folder contains a deleted charity tag and the name Bayou Night Logistics. That unanswered thread carries into Chapter 3.

seriesArc.nextThread: Chapter 3 follows the charity livestream trail.

lobbyBackstory: The first death at Pelican to Mars did not end the case. The Black Pelican sponsor file has opened a new trail: missing money, a camera export gap, and a manager who knows more than he is saying. Tonight, detectives are no longer solving one mistake at the bar — they are following the first signs of a cover-up.
